notable_excerpts: We have now 2.5 gigawatt. Anybody having more than one gigawatt in the renewable space is considered formidable.
We have planned to go up to 10 gigawatts. Today, we are 2.5 gigawatts. So, 7.5 gigawatts we have to add by 2030.
That being a fossil fuel company, we'll do our bit of job to see that world is greener and better.
We are also thankful to them for giving the best talents for enhancing the production of Western Offshore.
Production, in fact, the moment we are done with KG 98/2, the gas production will go up.
ONGC has compiled its financial results for the quarter and financial year ended 31st March 2025, which have been audited by the Statutory Auditors.
The Board has recommended final dividend of 25% that is INR 1.25 per share.

chain_of_thought: The environmental disclosures are strong, focusing on significant renewable energy expansion and commitment to reducing carbon emissions. Social aspects are moderately covered, with mentions of community impact and employee engagement. Governance is adequately addressed with transparency in financial reporting and risk management practices.
weaknesses: Limited specific details on social initiatives
